headline: Built for AirOps
subheadline: What happens when you give a GTM operator Claude Code and three months of obsession

challenges.title: The Interview Gap
challenges.desc: When we talked, I didn't have hands-on AI infrastructure experience. I knew the strategy, the GTM playbooks, the sales ops frameworks - but I hadn't built the systems myself. That gap was real.

challenges.title: What Changed
challenges.desc: Three months of building with Claude Code, shipping real infrastructure daily. GEO engines, content pipelines, AI-powered prospecting, signal detection - not theory, not demos. Production systems that run every day.

challenges.title: The GTM OS
challenges.desc: Built an entire go-to-market operating system from scratch. Three websites, a mission control dashboard, automated content pipelines, CRM integrations, and an AI assistant that learns and evolves. All open, all documented.

challenges.title: Why This Matters for AirOps
challenges.desc: Your platform helps brands win in AI search. I'm living that problem daily - building the exact GEO and content infrastructure your customers need. Not as a marketer pitching it. As a practitioner shipping it.

deliverables.title: GEO Engine
deliverables.desc: Full generative engine optimization pipeline. Content that's structured for AI discovery, not just traditional search. Automated publishing, signal tracking, brand presence monitoring across LLM outputs.

deliverables.title: Content OS
deliverables.desc: Multi-channel content system - blog posts, LinkedIn, X, Substack - all generated with voice rules, anti-slop filters, and brand DNA baked in. Not generic AI content. Content that sounds like a human wrote it because the system knows the voice.

deliverables.title: ABM Pipeline
deliverables.desc: The system that built this page. Exa for company research, Apollo for contacts, Grok for personalized copy, dynamic Next.js pages deployed at scale. 100 personalized landing pages from a single command.

deliverables.title: AI Sales Infrastructure
deliverables.desc: Signal detection, enrichment pipelines, automated prospecting, engagement tracking. The full stack that turns research into conversations - built on the same AI-first principles AirOps champions.

engagementSteps.title: Expert / Content Engineer
engagementSteps.subtitle: The Idea
engagementSteps.desc: I've been building exactly what AirOps customers need - GEO engines, content pipelines, AI search optimization. Happy to contribute as an expert, content engineer, or however it makes sense. The work speaks for itself.

engagementSteps.title: Community & Content
engagementSteps.subtitle: Another Option
engagementSteps.desc: Your cohort program and content engineer community is growing fast. I'm documenting everything I build publicly. Could be a natural fit for case studies, workshops, or community contributions.

engagementSteps.title: Just Staying Connected
engagementSteps.subtitle: At Minimum
engagementSteps.desc: No pressure on any of this. Genuinely just wanted to show you what I've been building since we talked. The interview was a catalyst - pushed me to close the gap and then some.

faqItems.question: What's the GTM OS?
faqItems.answer: An open-source go-to-market operating system. Three Next.js sites (thegtmos.ai, shawnos.ai, thecontentos.ai), a mission control dashboard, AI-powered content pipelines, CRM, and an evolving AI assistant called Nio. All built solo with Claude Code.

faqItems.question: What does the GEO engine actually do?
faqItems.answer: Monitors how brands appear in AI-generated responses (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity). Tracks citations, optimizes content structure for AI discovery, and publishes content designed to surface in LLM outputs - not just Google results.

faqItems.question: How does the content pipeline work?
faqItems.answer: Voice rules and anti-slop filters feed into every piece of content. The system knows Shawn's writing style, enforces it across channels, and auto-publishes to LinkedIn, X, Substack, and the blog. No generic AI voice.

faqItems.question: Is this all actually running in production?
faqItems.answer: Everything. Daily crons, live websites, real content publishing, actual CRM data. Not a prototype or pitch deck. Ship logs going back three months at thegtmos.ai/log.

faqItems.question: What's the tech stack?
faqItems.answer: TypeScript (Next.js, React, Remotion), Python (automation scripts), Claude Code (Opus), Grok, Exa, Apollo, PostHog, Cloudflare, Vercel. Local Ollama models for high-frequency crons.

faqItems.question: Why reach out now?
faqItems.answer: Because the gap that existed during our interview doesn't exist anymore. I've spent three months building the exact infrastructure AirOps helps companies create. Felt like you'd appreciate seeing it.
